ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for wholegrain cereal with fruits or nuts served with milk; closest match is the general cereal food product class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Grain milling products — wholegrain cereal-based composite dish with milk, fruits and nuts; mapped to grain milling products category as closest FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for this composite dish; A000E (grain milling products) is the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite cereal dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately 250 g (one bowl), consisting of about 40–50 g of wholegrain cereal with fruits or nuts topped with approximately 200 ml of plain milk.

nutrient_source.title: Wholegrain cereal with fruits or nuts with milk — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (F05050033)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database record F05050033 for wholegrain cereal with fruits or nuts with milk. All per-100g values are as reported. Saturated fat is not separately reported in the HPB record; value estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entry (USDA SR Legacy: Cereals, ready-to-eat, with raisins, nuts, and honey, NDB 08013) and scaled to match the HPB composite fat profile.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for muesli-type wholegrain cereal with milk
gi_source.source_match_notes: No exact GI entry exists for this specific HPB composite food. GI of 66 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for muesli-type wholegrain cereal served with full-fat milk (GI range 55–70 reported across multiple studies; median 66 used). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (66) × carbohydrate per serving (41.6 g for 250 g serving) / 100 = 27.5. This is classified as medium GI (56–69 range).

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sugar content (7.87 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.12 g/100g) and very low potassium (11 mg/100g), limiting cardiovascular buffering capacity
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should choose unsweetened wholegrain cereal varieties to reduce sugar-driven insulin and sodium-retention effects, and add fresh fruit to increase potassium intake, given that the potassium content of this dish (11 mg/100g) is insufficient to offset sodium-related blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~66) with moderate sugar (7.87 g/100g) and low dietary fibre (1.12 g/100g), resulting in a high glycemic load per serving (~27.5)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should limit portion size to reduce glycemic load, select no-added-sugar wholegrain cereal varieties, and consider replacing plain milk with unsweetened Greek yogurt to increase protein content and slow postprandial glucose absorption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low sodium (62 mg/100g), moderate fat (5.14 g/100g), and wholegrain base providing some beta-glucan fibre, with calcium (118 mg/100g) supporting vascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is broadly acceptable for cardiovascular health when prepared with unsweetened wholegrain cereal and low-fat milk, as the low sodium and moderate fat profile are consistent with heart-healthy dietary patterns, though the low fibre content (1.12 g/100g) limits the cholesterol-lowering benefit typically associated with wholegrain foods.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wholegrain cereal with fruits or nuts with milk is a ready-to-eat breakfast dish consisting of wholegrain cereal (such as oats, wheat flakes, or a multigrain blend) combined with dried or fresh fruits and nuts, served with plain cow's milk. The dish requires no cooking and is assembled by pouring milk over the cereal mixture in a bowl. The texture is a combination of chewy grains, crunchy nuts, and soft fruit pieces softened by the milk, with a mildly sweet flavour from the fruit and a creamy background from the milk. It is a common breakfast and snack option in Singapore and across Western-influenced food cultures. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 131 kcal, 4.29 g protein, 5.14 g fat, 16.81 g carbohydrate, 7.87 g sugar, 1.12 g dietary fibre, 62 mg sodium, and 118 mg calcium. The calcium content is notably high due to the milk component, supporting bone health. The glycemic index is approximately 66 (medium), yielding a high glycemic load per standard 250 g serving (~27.5), which is clinically relevant for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or insulin resistance. The low dietary fibre content relative to carbohydrate means glucose absorption is not strongly buffered. For hypertension management, the very low potassium (11 mg/100g) is a limitation. The dish is vegetarian and halal-suitable but contains gluten, milk, and tree nut allergens.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database record F05050033 (confidence 0.95 as reported in input). The food is an exact match in the HPB database. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous entry and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for muesli-type wholegrain cereal with milk, which is the closest defensible approved-source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database record F05050033, and no sufficiently close approved-source equivalent was available to provide defensible estimates for all micronutrients. Saturated fat (1.8 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for ready-to-eat cereal with raisins, nuts, and honey (NDB 08013), scaled to match the HPB composite fat profile of 5.14 g/100g total fat; this is documented as an adapted value. Potassium is reported as 11 mg/100g in the HPB source, which is unusually low for a milk-containing dish; this may reflect the specific cereal-to-milk ratio used in the HPB composite. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for muesli-type wholegrain cereal with milk. All core macros (protein_g 4.29, fat_g 5.14, carbohydrate_g 16.81) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification is required.
